Package i5.cb.graph.diagram

Interface DiagramObject

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    DiagramEdge, DiagramNode

    public interface DiagramObject
    This interface defines of diagram objects, i.e. nodes as well as edges. The user object that has to be specified in the constructors of the subclasses can be any kind of Java object. The look and feel of the GraphEditor is determined by the DiagramClass, which constructs the components that are used to display the user objects. DiagramObjects can be identified by the user objects.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void addEdge​(DiagramEdge e)
      Makes this DiagramObject the source or destination of another DiagramEdge
      void erase​()
      Erases this DiagramObject from its DiagramDesktop
      DiagramClass getDiagramClass​()
      Gets the DiagramClass that manages all associations of this DiagramObject's userObject.
      DiagramDesktop getDiagramDesktop​()
      Gets the DiagramDesktop this DiagramObject is currently shown in.
      java.util.Vector getEdges​()
      Gets the DiagramEdges this DiagramObject is source or destination of
      DiagramNode getNode​()
      It's often necessary to get the node, mo matter if it sits on an edge or not
      java.awt.Shape getShape​()
      Gets the shape associated with this DiagramObject's userObject.
      java.lang.Object getUserObject​()
      Returns the userobject this DiagramObject is associated with.
      boolean hasFixedPosition​()
      Determines if this DiagramObject has a fixed position on the DiagramDesktop.
      boolean isComponentVisible​()
      Determine if this DiagramObject's Component is currently visible.
      boolean isSmallComponentVisible​()
      Determine if this DiagramObject's smallComponent is currently visible.
      void paint​(java.awt.Graphics g)
      Every object implementing this inferface should have a paint method as we are dealing with a graphical representation of objects and their relations.
      boolean removeEdge​(DiagramEdge e)
      Removes a certain edge from the collection of those this DiagramObject is source or destintion of.
      boolean setComponentVisible​()
      Set this DiagramObject's Component visible.
      boolean setSmallComponentVisible​()
      Set this DiagramObject's smallComponent visible.

    • Method Detail

      • getUserObject

        java.lang.Object getUserObject​()
        Returns the userobject this DiagramObject is associated with.
        Returns:
        the userobject this DiagramObject is associated with. Any Object may be associated with a DiagramObject

      • getNode

        DiagramNode getNode​()
        It's often necessary to get the node, mo matter if it sits on an edge or not

      • getDiagramClass

        DiagramClass getDiagramClass​()
        Gets the DiagramClass that manages all associations of this DiagramObject's userObject.
        Returns:
        a DiagramClass value

      • getDiagramDesktop

        DiagramDesktop getDiagramDesktop​()
        Gets the DiagramDesktop this DiagramObject is currently shown in.
        Returns:
        a DiagramDesktop value

      • addEdge

        void addEdge​(DiagramEdge e)
        Makes this DiagramObject the source or destination of another DiagramEdge
        Parameters:
        e - a DiagramEdge value

      • removeEdge

        boolean removeEdge​(DiagramEdge e)
        Removes a certain edge from the collection of those this DiagramObject is source or destintion of.
        Parameters:
        e - the DiagramEdge to be removed
        Returns:
        a boolean value that can tell the called whether the operation was successfull

      • getEdges

        java.util.Vector getEdges​()
        Gets the DiagramEdges this DiagramObject is source or destination of
        Returns:
        a Vector value

      • erase

        void erase​()
        Erases this DiagramObject from its DiagramDesktop

      • isComponentVisible

        boolean isComponentVisible​()
        Determine if this DiagramObject's Component is currently visible. Only one of the components "component" and smallComponent" can be visible at the same time.
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • isSmallComponentVisible

        boolean isSmallComponentVisible​()
        Determine if this DiagramObject's smallComponent is currently visible. Only one of the components "component" and smallComponent" can be visible at the same time.
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • setComponentVisible

        boolean setComponentVisible​()
        Set this DiagramObject's Component visible. Only one of the components "component" and smallComponent" can be visible at the same time. So setting the one visible means to set the other one visible.
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • setSmallComponentVisible

        boolean setSmallComponentVisible​()
        Set this DiagramObject's smallComponent visible. Only one of the components "component" and smallComponent" can be visible at the same time. So setting the one visible means to set the other one visible.
        Returns:
        a boolean value

      • paint

        void paint​(java.awt.Graphics g)
        Every object implementing this inferface should have a paint method as we are dealing with a graphical representation of objects and their relations. Using DiagramObjects without somehow painting them doesn't seem to make sense.
        Parameters:
        g - a Graphics value

      • getShape

        java.awt.Shape getShape​()
        Gets the shape associated with this DiagramObject's userObject.
        Returns:
        a Shape value

      • hasFixedPosition

        boolean hasFixedPosition​()
        Determines if this DiagramObject has a fixed position on the DiagramDesktop. This member's value is true for DiagramEdges for example as they are not to be removed directly but by removing their source- and destinationnode and the node sitting on it. I really like long sentences.
        Returns:
        a boolean value
